description_long: This object is a vintage Weston A.C. Milliammeter radio receiver, featuring a rectangular wooden case with a weathered, textured finish. The front panel displays a large, semi-circular analog meter with a glass cover, marked with measurement increments and the label 'WESTON A.C. MILLIAMMETER.' Three knurled metal knobs are positioned on the top, likely for calibration or adjustment, while a black metal plate and a rotary switch are mounted below the meter. The sides of the case include a sturdy leather carrying handle attached with metal brackets, and the back is plain wood with visible wear and four mounting screws. The overall construction suggests a robust, utilitarian design typical of early to mid-20th-century electrical instruments.
description: A vintage wooden Weston A.C. Milliammeter radio receiver with a large analog meter, three top knobs, and a leather side handle. The case is weathered and features metal and glass components.
description_view_0: The front view shows the analog meter, three top knobs, a black metal plate, and a rotary switch, all set in a worn wooden case.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the leather carrying handle secured by metal brackets and the thickness of the wooden case.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a plain, weathered wooden surface with four mounting screws and no visible controls or features.
description_view_3: The right side view shows the opposite side of the case, with visible screws and the edge of the top knobs, but no handle.
